title: How I Know David Grusch Is Not Lying…
summary: Jesse Michels summarizes key points from his documentary with David Grusch, a former intelligence officer who testified about covert UFO crash retrieval programs. The video explores connections between UFOs and nuclear programs, historical debunking efforts, anti-gravity research, and calls for more serious investigation.

acronyms.definition: Long-range nuclear missiles monitored by launch officers
A screening program for personnel with access to nuclear weapons requiring them to report any medications or personal issues

acronyms.expansion: National Investigations Committee on Aerial Phenomena
acronyms.definition: The first civilian UFO study group, formed by Townsend Brown
A secure room where classified information can be discussed, requiring leaving phones and electronic devices outside

events.description: The Navy fires Townsend Brown without cause
McMahon Atomic Secrecy Act drafted, predecessor to 1954 Atomic Energy Act
Guy Hottel memo describing crashed UFO with alien crew becomes most read document on FBI website
CIA Director Walter B. Smith writes about using UFO phenomena for psychological warfare against Soviets
Atomic Energy Act passed, which Grusch claims was written to protect UFO secrets
Document allegedly showing JFK asking CIA Director about unknowns in space, dated November 12th, 10 days before his death
Edward Condon's clearance mysteriously restored and he's placed on panel that deals death blow to UFO research
Document supposedly released under FOIA showing JFK asking about unknowns in space

key_claims.claim: David Grusch brought 40 people who have handled exotic UFO material firsthand to the Intelligence Community's Inspector General
The 1954 Atomic Energy Act was deliberately written to protect UFO secrets using nuclear secrecy provisions
The Manhattan Project was basically the original UFO program and received UFO reports
The B2 stealth bomber uses Townsend Brown's electric gravitics technology
Roswell crash materials were reportedly taken to Wright Patterson Air Force Base, which became an anti-gravity research center in the 1950s
E. Howard Hunt claimed JFK's death had to do partially with his inquiries into UFOs
Department of Energy and Manhattan Project clearance system is most applicable to UFOs, not clearances that roll up to the Executive Branch
Lockheed Martin's response to Grusch's testimony was that questions about UAPs are best answered by the federal government
